@byalexai Thank you! I'm pretty frequently using Claude Code to start and iterate on ideas, research datasets, etc and it's so nice to be able to, at the end of that process, push everything into Retool and not worry about how I'm going to give my team access to all that work.  
  
Thanks for the support!

EverTutor AI

EverTutor AI

Most AI app builders stop at generating code.

Retool seems to be solving the harder problem: getting from an idea to software that can actually be deployed, governed, and trusted inside a company.

Curious how many teams here are already using AI-generated apps in production today? 👀

results.title: UI Bakery Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

 6 views

•1 review

I originally started with 3 Low-Code Solutions for our application: 1) Retool 2) Appsmith 3) UI.Bakery Myself personally, I created the application in literally all 3 before choosing my favorite. Reasons for my selection: 1) Price...clear and simple...no price per user, no price per database call, etc...the competitors are VERY quick to hide these details. 2) UI...we presented our demo to quite a few people, and UI Bakery stood out as the clear winner 3) Customer Support...I have never quite met a company that is SO engaged. The chat window is INSTANT. Meeting requests are very fast and often the next day 4) This tool can be used for simple applications or complicated ones (such as mine with heavy Javascript coding). It really covers it all. [...] versatile application use(2)

Based on 6 reviews

Review UI Bakery?

UI Bakery receives high praise for its ability to significantly accelerate frontend development, with users noting a 3x speed increase in custom system development. Makers of various products highlight its advanced low-code capabilities, ease of use, and exceptional customer support. The tool is appreciated for its clear pricing model and flexibility in handling both simple and complex applications. Users commend the responsive support team and frequent feature updates, making it a highly recommended choice for building web-based applications efficiently.

results.title: Bubble Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
#### What needs improvement

Performance. Bubble apps feel sluggish compared to coded alternatives, especially on mobile. The learning curve is steeper than advertised. API integrations work but feel clunky beyond basic REST. Responsive design needs manual tweaking for every breakpoint. And once you hit serious traffic, you start fighting the platform instead of building on it.

#### vs Alternatives

Webflow
Codex by OpenAI
Claude Code

Webflow for frontend-heavy sites, Adalo and FlutterFlow for mobile-first apps, Retool for internal tools. Bubble won on flexibility for complex logic and database relationships. The trade-off is that migrating to real code later means a full rebuild.

Nolan Vu

#### What's great [...] Mobile responsiveness is still the weakest spot. Getting the layout to behave across different screen sizes requires a lot of manual tweaking that feels like it should just work by now. For web apps it's solid, for anything mobile-first I'd look elsewhere.

#### What's great

Bubble lets you build a full web app with auth, database and workflows without writing code. For non-technical founders validating an idea, the speed from concept to live product is unmatched. The visual editor is powerful, the plugin ecosystem covers most integrations, and it's far cheaper than hiring a dev team for an MVP.
